headings.text: Applying Threat Intelligence in SOC Operations

overview: Threat Intelligence (TI) plays a critical role in modern Security Operations Centers (SOCs). It empowers defenders to anticipate, detect, and respond to cyber threats more effectively by transforming raw data into actionable insights. In SOC environments, TI helps streamline detection engineering, automate threat hunting, and enrich incident response by prov

study.overview: Threat Intelligence (TI) plays a critical role in modern Security Operations Centers (SOCs). It empowers defenders to anticipate, detect, and respond to cyber threats more effectively by transforming raw data into actionable insights. In SOC environments, TI helps streamline detection engineering, automate threat hunting, and enrich incident response by prov
study.whySoc: Use this note as an analyst reference: understand the concept, identify the relevant telemetry, pivot through evidence, and document the decision path clearly.
keyConcepts: Applying Threat Intelligence in SOC Operations
workflow: Identify the user, host, process, network flow, or identity object involved.
Collect the highest-value logs first and preserve timestamps and assumptions.
Pivot through related notes, telemetry sources, and MITRE references.
Document findings, gaps, containment options, and detection improvements.
tips: Example: A SOC receives updated phishing IOCs from MISP and adds them to email gateway rules and DNS sinkhole lists.
Tip: Select feeds that align with your industry’s specific threat landscape, not generic “noise.”
